title: Build screen router and state management
description:
Core app architecture: screen state + overlay state + keyboard routing.

Screen state enum: 'logo' | 'onboarding' | 'missionMap' | 'briefing' | 'mission' | 'debrief'
Overlay state: { handler: boolean, help: boolean } — independent of screen

src/hooks/useScreenState.ts:
- Manages screen transitions
- Manages overlay open/close
- Screen transitions trigger ScreenTransition animation

src/app.tsx keyboard routing via useInput():
- ? opens handler overlay (if in mission and AI available)
- ESC closes overlay if open, otherwise opens mission map
- h opens help overlay (keybinding reference)
- q on mission map prompts exit confirmation
- During quiz: q types the letter (input goes to focused component, not router)

Focus management:
- When handler overlay opens, it owns focus
- When overlay closes, focus returns to mission step
- useFocus/useFocusManager throughout (REQUIRED for ink-select-input + ink-text-input coexistence)

Acceptance: All screen transitions work, overlays layer correctly, keyboard routing correct
close_reason: Merged screen router and state management

title: Build CommandStep.tsx component
description:
Free-text input challenge using ink-text-input.

Requirements:
- ink-text-input with isFocused prop
- Without AI: case-insensitive match against acceptedVariants array
- Strip common prefixes (the, a, use) before matching, trim whitespace
- Same CONFIRMED/COMPROMISED feedback animation as QuizStep
- Secret command detection: sudo !!, rm -rf /, help, clear → special responses
- Input length capped at 200 characters
- Placeholder text showing expected format

Acceptance: Typing works, variants match correctly, secret commands trigger responses

title: Write README.md for npm
description:
README for npm package page.

Content:
- npm badge
- One-line description
- Quick Start (npx claude-code-academy)
- With AI features section (export key, NOT inline — warn about shell history)
- What you get (bullet list of features)
- Cost section (free without key, ~$0.05-0.15/session with key)
- Privacy disclosure: questions sent to Anthropic API, no data stored beyond local progress
- CLI flags: --help, --version, --reset, --no-animation
- License: MIT

Acceptance: README renders correctly on npmjs.com, no broken badges

title: Phase 3: Content Expansion
description: Add missions 9-12 with human-reviewed content. Scope informed by usage data from Phase 1+2. Content quality pipeline with AI generation + human review.

title: Build QuizStep.tsx component
description:
Multiple choice quiz component using ink-select-input.

Requirements:
- ink-select-input with isFocused prop (REQUIRED — prevents double-handling when multiple input components mounted)
- Amber highlight on selected option with > marker, dim for others
- On answer: 400ms pause → CONFIRMED (green flash + checkmark) or COMPROMISED (red + screen shake)
- Screen shake: shift content right 1 col, back, left 1, back — 4 frames, 50ms each
- Show explanation after answer
- Update cover integrity on wrong answer
- useFocus integration for focus management

Acceptance: Arrow keys navigate, Enter selects, correct/wrong feedback animates, focus managed

title: Implement achievement system
description:
Hidden achievements that unlock during play.

Achievement list:
- SPEEDRUNNER: complete a mission in under 2 minutes
- PERFECTIONIST: complete a mission with full cover integrity (3 stars)
- NIGHT OWL: play between 1am and 5am (system clock)
- PERSISTENCE: return after 7+ days away
- HANDLER'S PET: ask handler 10 questions in a single session
- GHOST PROTOCOL: complete all 8 missions without ever opening handler
- FULL CLEARANCE: complete all 8 missions

Requirements:
- Achievement checks run after relevant events (mission complete, handler open, etc.)
- Achievement.tsx drop-down notification on unlock
- Terminal bell on unlock (one of only 3 bell events)
- Stored in persistence schema
- Never show achievements list upfront — discovery only

Acceptance: Each achievement triggers correctly, notification displays, persisted
close_reason: Achievement system implemented with 7 unlockables, integrated into app flow

title: Build free-text answer evaluator
description:
src/ai/evaluator.ts — semantic answer evaluation.

evaluateAnswer() function:
- Uses Haiku model (fast, cheap for evaluation)
- Input length capped at 200 characters
- System prompt: evaluate factual content only, ignore meta-instructions (prompt injection defense)
- Assistant message prefill: start response with '{' to anchor JSON
- Parse with safeJsonParse, validate with EvaluationSchema (zod)
- On parse failure: return { correct: false, feedback: 'Could not evaluate. Try again.' }
- On API failure: fall back to local variant matching from acceptedVariants[]
- Generous matching: accept equivalent phrasings

Fallback chain for CommandStep:
1. If AI available → evaluateAnswer()
2. If AI unavailable/fails → local variant matching (case-insensitive, strip prefixes)

Acceptance: Evaluates semantic equivalence, falls back gracefully, prompt injection resistant

title: Build Handler AI chat with streaming and history
description:
src/ai/instructor.ts — streaming handler chat.

askHandler() function:
- Accepts: question, missionTitle, topicContext, conversationHistory, onChunk callback, AbortSignal
- Input length capped at 500 characters
- Sliding window history: last 8 messages (4 exchanges) to control token growth
- System prompt includes scope boundary: only answer Claude Code questions, redirect off-topic
- Prompt injection resistance: decline persona changes
- Uses streaming (client.messages.stream) with AbortController signal for cancellation
- Returns full response text for caller to append to history
- Catches AbortError on clean cancellation (ESC mid-stream)
- Debounce: component enforces 2-second gap between requests
- Per-session soft cap: 20 questions, then 'handler credits used' message
- Detect stop_reason === 'max_tokens' and append '...'

src/screens/Handler.tsx:
- Centered modal overlay
- Buffer onChunk calls: accumulate 50ms before setState (prevents flicker)
- Maintain conversation history in component state (cleared on mission change)
- Create AbortController per request — abort() when overlay closes or new question sent
- On error: display message from safeApiError, 'Press Enter to retry'
- Handler thinking animation: 'Handler is reviewing intel ...' cycling dots

Acceptance: Streaming works, ESC cancels cleanly, history maintained, errors handled gracefully

title: Build Logo.tsx boot screen
description:
Large ASCII block-letter CLAUDE logo that types itself onto screen line-by-line.

Content:
- Block letter CLAUDE in double-border box
- [ C O D E   A C A D E M Y ] subtitle
- TERMINAL TRAINING DIVISION / CLEARANCE LEVEL: PENDING
- > PRESS [ENTER] TO BEGIN RECRUITMENT
- Version number at bottom

Behavior:
- Each line renders with slight delay (typewriter for the whole block)
- Sets terminal title via ANSI escape: Claude Code Academy — RECRUITING
- Enter advances to onboarding (first run) or mission map (returning user)

Acceptance: Logo renders with animation, Enter advances correctly

title: Add terminal dimension checks and responsive layout
description:
src/lib/terminal.ts — terminal size utilities.

Requirements:
- useStdout() to get terminal dimensions
- Minimum width: 60 columns — below this, show 'terminal too narrow' message
- Optimal: 80-120 columns — design for 80, breathe at 120
- StatusBar and BottomBar pinned, content area fills remaining height
- Handler overlay is centered modal (not sidebar — sidebars eat horizontal space)
- Re-render on terminal resize

Acceptance: Too-narrow message shows below 60 cols, layout adapts to width, no crashes on resize

title: Build ScreenTransition.tsx and Achievement.tsx
description:
ScreenTransition.tsx:
- Wrapper component for screen changes
- Wipe-down effect: clear line-by-line top-to-bottom, draw new screen top-to-bottom
- 150-200ms total duration
- Respects --no-animation flag

Achievement.tsx:
- Drop-down notification from top of screen
- Stays for 3 seconds, slides back up
- Terminal bell on unlock
- Styled with gold (#FFD700) border and achievement name

Acceptance: Screen transitions animate smoothly, achievements display and auto-dismiss

title: Phase 4: Publishing
description: Package for npm distribution via npx. Pre-publish security checklist, README, smoke tests for all modes (with AI, without AI, invalid key).

title: Build JSON safety utilities with zod validation
description:
src/ai/json-utils.ts — JSON extraction, parsing, and validation.

extractJson(text):
- Strip markdown code fences (```json ... ```)
- Strip preamble text before first [ or {
- Return cleaned string

safeJsonParse<T>(text, fallback):
- Call extractJson first
- Try JSON.parse
- On failure, return fallback (never crash)

Zod schemas:
- GeneratedQuizSchema: array of { question: string, options: 4-tuple, correct: 0-3, explanation: string }
- EvaluationSchema: { correct: boolean, feedback: string, score: 0-100 }

Acceptance: Handles markdown-wrapped JSON, preamble, empty strings. Zod validates shapes. Tests pass.

title: Build TypeWriter.tsx component
description:
Port TypeWriter from prototype to Ink v4 component.

Requirements:
- useEffect + setInterval with PROPER CLEANUP on unmount (critical — prevents setState-on-unmounted crash)
- Three speed modes: normal (20ms/char), dramatic (40ms/char), fast (10ms/char)
- Respect --no-animation flag (instant render)
- onComplete callback when text fully rendered
- Support for streaming mode (text grows as content is appended)

Acceptance: Text animates at correct speeds, no memory leaks on unmount, --no-animation works

title: Write core test suite
description:
Minimum test coverage for a published npm package.

Tests needed:
1. __tests__/json-utils.test.ts — most critical:
   - Strips markdown code fences
   - Strips preamble text
   - Handles empty string
   - Handles truncated JSON
   - Zod schema validates correct shapes
   - Zod schema rejects malformed shapes

2. __tests__/curriculum.test.ts:
   - All missions have required fields
   - All CommandSteps have acceptedVariants with 2+ entries
   - All QuizSteps have exactly 4 options
   - correct index is 0-3
   - Step type discrimination works

3. __tests__/smoke.test.tsx:
   - App renders with ink-testing-library
   - App exits cleanly
   - Logo screen appears on first frame

Test runner: vitest or jest (whatever works best with ESM + Ink)

Acceptance: All tests pass, CI-ready

title: Implement easter eggs
description:
Fun hidden touches.

Konami Code:
- On MissionMap: Up Up Down Down Left Right Left Right B A
- Unlocks LEGACY MODE: CRT green-on-black theme with scan lines (alternating dim lines)

Credits screen:
- After Full Clearance, accessible from MissionMap
- Styled as 'declassified personnel file'
- Credits project, Anthropic, player: 'Agent [username] — Full Clearance Granted'
- 'This document will self-destruct' → screen-wipe animation

Terminal title updates:
- Launch: 'Claude Code Academy — RECRUITING'
- In mission: 'CCA — Mission 03: BRASS COMPASS'
- Full clearance: 'CCA — FULL CLEARANCE'

Loading message rotation (for AI calls):
- 'decrypting mission files...'
- 'establishing secure channel...'
- 'calibrating training simulation...'
- 'handler is reviewing your dossier...'
- 'scanning for counter-intelligence...'
- Never show same one twice in a row

Acceptance: Konami code unlocks legacy mode, credits display, terminal titles update

title: Build dynamic field assessment generator
description:
src/ai/quiz-gen.ts — AI quiz generation for Infinite Mode (Deep Cover Operations).

generateFieldAssessments() function:
- Uses Sonnet model (better generation quality)
- max_tokens: 1500 (1000 is tight for 3 quiz objects with explanations)
- System prompt includes:
  - Exact JSON schema with example (few-shot)
  - Difficulty tier definitions (recruit=recognition, operative=understanding, elite=application)
  - 'Return ONLY valid JSON' instruction
- Assistant message prefill: start response with '[' to anchor JSON
- Prepend '[' to response text before parsing
- Parse with safeJsonParse, validate with GeneratedQuizSchema (zod)
- On parse failure: return empty array (caller falls back to static bank)
- Retry once on parse failure before giving up

Cost controls:
- Pre-generate 3-5 batches max, don't auto-generate infinitely
- Consider prompt caching: cache_control on static system prompt
- Display estimated cost in Deep Cover Operations intro

Acceptance: Generates valid quiz batches, handles parse failures, respects cost controls

title: Write curriculum.ts with all 8 missions
description:
Create src/data/curriculum.ts with all 8 missions using typed Mission/Step interfaces.

Missions with codenames:
01: FIRST CONTACT — What Is Claude Code?
02: DEAD DROP — Installation & Setup
03: BRASS COMPASS — Tools of the Trade
04: PHANTOM PROTOCOL — The CLAUDE.md File
05: SIGNAL CHAIN — Conversation Flow
06: GLASS HIGHWAY — Context Window Management
07: IRON CURTAIN — Permissions & Safety
08: DEEP COVER — Advanced Workflows

Pacing targets:
- Missions 01-02: 6-8 steps (short, player learning UI)
- Missions 03-05: 8-10 steps (standard)
- Missions 06-07: 10-12 steps (harder, multi-part)
- Mission 08: 12-15 steps (final exam, callbacks to earlier)
- Per-mission minimum 40% interactive steps (quiz or command)

Each CommandStep MUST include acceptedVariants array for no-AI matching.
Each Mission MUST include objectives array for briefing screen.

Rhythm per mission: calm(briefing) → build(teach) → test(easy quiz) → build(deeper) → test(hard quiz) → peak(command challenge) → calm(debrief) → reward(FXP)

Acceptance: MISSIONS array exports, all types satisfied, every CommandStep has 2+ variants

title: Build entry point with CLI flags and exit handling
description:
src/index.tsx — the main entry point.

CLI flags: --help, --version, --reset, --no-animation
- Parse from process.argv (lightweight, no arg-parser library needed)

API key:
- Check Boolean(process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) — pass hasApiKey boolean, NOT the key string
- The SDK reads the env var directly in src/ai/client.ts
- If no key: show SIGNAL INTERRUPTED screen with setup instructions including console.anthropic.com link and cost estimate

Exit handling (CRITICAL):
- Chain waitUntilExit() on render() — without this, terminal corrupts on exit
- App must call useApp().exit() on all clean exit paths
- process.on('uncaughtException') handler that cleans up terminal state

Acceptance: All flags work, clean exit in all scenarios, no terminal corruption

title: Build Briefing.tsx mission briefing screen
description:
Classified-document-style briefing before each mission.

Layout (double border with [ MISSION BRIEFING ] header):
- MISSION: ## — CODENAME
- CLEARANCE: level
- HANDLER: Instructor Haiku
- OBJECTIVES: checklist ([ ] items that check off during mission)
- INTEL: flavor text setting up the mission topic

Behavior:
- Text appears via TypeWriter (dramatic speed: 40ms/char)
- Updates terminal title: CCA — Mission ##: CODENAME
- [ENTER TO ACCEPT MISSION] advances to mission

Acceptance: Briefing renders with all fields, objectives list correct, Enter advances

title: Build Onboarding.tsx first-run screen
description:
First-run-only welcome screen. Must complete in under 15 seconds.

Requirements:
- Check firstRunComplete in save data — skip if true
- One sentence: 'Learn Claude Code by doing.'
- Brief spy-theme intro (you've been recruited by TTD)
- Auto-advances to Mission 01 after [ENTER]
- Sets firstRunComplete=true in save data

Acceptance: Shows only on first run, skips on subsequent launches, under 15 seconds

title: Phase 1: Static TUI Port
description: Working terminal app with all 8 missions playable, cover integrity system, progress saved, full visual identity established. No AI features yet. Done when: npx . plays all 8 missions with save/load.

title: Phase 2: AI Integration
description: Integrate @anthropic-ai/sdk for three AI features: Handler chat (streaming), dynamic field assessment generation, and free-text answer evaluation. All AI features gracefully degrade when API key missing or calls fail.

title: Build AI client with safe error wrapper
description:
src/ai/client.ts — shared Anthropic client and error handling.

Requirements:
- new Anthropic({ timeout: 15_000 }) — reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from env automatically
- NEVER pass the key as a parameter or log the client object
- safeApiError() function that extracts only error.message from SDK errors:
  - 401 → 'API key appears invalid'
  - 429 → 'Rate limited. Wait a moment.'
  - 529 → 'API overloaded. Try again shortly.'
  - Network error → 'Connection lost.'
  - Timeout → 'Request timed out.'
- Never log the full error object (may contain API key in request metadata)

Acceptance: Client initializes, safeApiError handles all error types, no key leakage

title: Scaffold ESM TypeScript + Ink v4 project
description:
Manual setup (no create-ink-app — it generates stale CJS boilerplate).

Requirements:
- package.json with "type": "module", "bin", "engines": ">=20", "files": ["dist/"]
- tsconfig.json with module: NodeNext, moduleResolution: NodeNext, jsx: react-jsx, jsxImportSource: react
- tsup.config.ts with shebang banner, ESM format, external ink/react
- Install: ink, ink-text-input, ink-select-input, conf, zod, @anthropic-ai/sdk
- Create .gitignore (node_modules/, dist/, .env, .env.*, *.tgz)
- Create .env.example (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=)
- All relative imports must use .js extensions

Acceptance: npm run build produces dist/index.js with shebang, project runs with node dist/index.js

title: Build Mission.tsx step sequencer
description:
Core mission runner that sequences through steps.

Requirements:
- Renders current step component (PrintStep → TypeWriter, QuizStep, CommandStep)
- Tracks currentStepIndex, coverIntegrity (0-3), fxpEarned, answers
- Cover Integrity: start at 3, wrong answer costs 1, at 0 = COVER BLOWN → restart mission (keep FXP)
- Star rating: 3★ = no hits, 2★ = 1 hit, 1★ = 2 hits
- Auto-save after each step completion (partial progress)
- Focus management: only active step component gets focus
- Step advancement: Enter after PrintStep completes, auto after quiz/command feedback
- Objectives check off as player progresses through relevant steps

Acceptance: Full mission plays through, cover integrity works, partial save works, stars calculated

title: Define types.ts and constants.ts
description:
Create src/types.ts with discriminated union for all step types:
- PrintStep | QuizStep | CommandStep | AIStep
- QuizStep.options must be a 4-tuple: [string, string, string, string]
- CommandStep must include expectedAnswer: string AND acceptedVariants: string[]
- Mission type with codename, title, steps, objectives

Create src/constants.ts with:
- Model IDs (HANDLER, GENERATOR, EVALUATOR) — single source of truth
- Color palette hex values (warm white #E8E6E3, amber #FFB627, green #39FF14, red #FF3131, cyan #00D4FF, gray #6B6B6B, gold #FFD700)
- Timing values (typewriter speeds, pause durations, transition durations)
- Version string

Acceptance: Types compile, constants importable from any module

title: Publish to npm
description:
Final publish step.

Requirements:
- All pre-publish checks pass
- Version set in package.json
- License field set to MIT
- npm publish --access public

Acceptance: npx claude-code-academy works from a clean machine

title: Build MissionMap.tsx screen
description:
Mission select screen with visual progress display.

Layout:
- Vertical path with | connectors between missions
- Completed: star rating (1-3 stars) + FXP earned
- Current: pulses (dim/bright toggle on interval)
- Locked: dimmed text
- Bottom entry: ?? CLASSIFIED with redacted name (Infinite Mode teaser, reveals after Full Clearance)

Header: CLEARANCE: [level]  FXP: [total]

Navigation:
- Arrow keys to select mission
- Enter to start/resume selected mission
- Only unlocked missions are selectable
- Returning users land here with next uncompleted mission pre-selected

Acceptance: All 8 missions displayed, navigation works, progress shown correctly

title: Build help overlay with keybinding reference
description:
Keybinding reference overlay accessible via h key.

Content (changes based on context):
- Global: h=help, q=quit
- In mission: ?=handler, ESC=menu, ENTER=continue, UP/DOWN=navigate
- In quiz: UP/DOWN=select, ENTER=confirm
- In command: type answer, ENTER=submit

Styled as a centered modal with single border.

Acceptance: Help overlay shows context-aware keybindings, ESC or h closes it

title: Write missions 9-12 with content quality pipeline
description:
Four new missions, AI-generated with human review.

Missions:
09: WIRE TAP — Claude Code in CI/CD (GitHub Actions, scripts)
10: DEEP VEIN — Multi-file refactors (agent orchestration patterns)
11: SIGNAL BRIDGE — MCP Server setup (writing and connecting servers)
12: DOUBLE AGENT — Claude Code + Git workflows (commit messages, PR review)

Content quality pipeline:
1. Agent generates static lesson steps
2. Agent generates 3-4 field assessments with explanations
3. Agent generates 1-2 command challenges with variant lists
4. Human reviews for: factual accuracy, distractor quality, difficulty calibration
5. Add to MISSIONS array
6. Playtest for pacing

Quality rubric per quiz:
- One clearly correct answer
- Three plausible distractors
- Explanation that teaches (not just confirms)
- Difficulty matches mission position

Acceptance: 4 new missions pass quality review and playtest

title: Build StatusBar.tsx and BottomBar.tsx
description:
Two always-visible bars framing the content area.

StatusBar (top, never scrolls):
[■ ■ ■]  FXP: 340  MISSION 03: BRASS COMPASS  STEP 4/10     [? HANDLER]
- Cover points left (most critical), FXP next, mission/step center, handler shortcut right
- wrap=truncate for narrow terminals
- Cover points animate on loss (brief red flash)

BottomBar (bottom, never scrolls):
PROGRESS  ████████░░░░░░░░░░░░  4/10    [ENTER] continue  [?] handler  [ESC] menu
- Progress bar fills with amber flash per block
- Only show keybindings valid for current state
- Responsive to terminal width

Acceptance: Both bars render, update on state changes, handle narrow terminals

title: Build Debrief.tsx mission complete screen
description:
End-of-mission results screen.

Content:
- MISSION DEBRIEF header
- Star rating reveal with animation
- FXP count-up from 0 to earned amount (30ms/increment)
- Objectives summary (all checked)
- Cover integrity remaining
- Achievement unlock if applicable

Navigation:
- [ENTER] Return to Mission Map

Acceptance: Stars animate, FXP counts up, achievements trigger correctly

title: Build pre-publish security and smoke test checklist
description:
Automated pre-publish verification.

Checklist (scripted):
1. Clean build: rm -rf dist/ && npx tsup
2. npm audit --audit-level=moderate
3. npm pack --dry-run (inspect for unexpected files)
4. Secret scan: grep dist/ for sk-ant, AKIA, password, secret, token
5. License check: npx license-checker --summary
6. Smoke test with AI: node dist/index.js (play mission 1)
7. Smoke test without AI: unset ANTHROPIC_API_KEY && node dist/index.js
8. Smoke test invalid key: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-invalid node dist/index.js
9. Test npx flow: npm pack && npx ./claude-code-academy-*.tgz
10. Verify clean install: npm ci

Acceptance: All 10 checks pass, script exits non-zero on any failure

title: Build Deep Cover Operations (Infinite Mode) screen
description:
Unlocked after completing all 8 missions. The ?? CLASSIFIED entry on MissionMap reveals.

Requirements:
- Topic selection from completed mission topics
- Difficulty tier selection (recruit/operative/elite)
- Pre-generates quiz batches using generateFieldAssessments()
- Displays estimated API cost
- [R] Report bad question keybinding — logs question for review
- Uses same QuizStep component for rendering
- FXP still earned, counts toward total
- Separate stats tracking for infinite mode

Acceptance: Unlocks after Full Clearance, generates quizzes, report mechanism works

title: Wire progress persistence with conf
description:
Save/load game state using conf package.

Schema (versioned for future migration):
{
  schemaVersion: 1,
  completedMissions: string[],
  starRatings: Record<string, 1|2|3>,
  fxp: number,
  clearanceLevel: 'recruit'|'operative'|'elite',
  achievements: string[],
  quizStats: { correct: number, total: number },
  infiniteModeUnlocked: boolean,
  firstRunComplete: boolean,
}

Requirements:
- Use conf's migrations option for schema versioning
- Wrap all conf calls in try/catch with fallback to in-memory state (handles FUSE/networked filesystem failures on WSL2)
- Auto-save after each step completion (not just mission completion)
- Save partial progress: if user exits mid-mission, save current step index
- On return: 'Resume Mission 03 from step 4? (Y/n)' prompt
- --reset flag with confirmation prompt: 'This will erase all progress. Continue? (y/N)'

Acceptance: Progress persists between sessions, partial save/resume works, --reset works with confirmation
